name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: The Arizona Senate race just took an ugly turn >>
body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6a22/24c69fd9/13a81db96/1892e810/4130463571/VEsH/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFCM16N_digital_email_20180110_SinemaRR_inact&source=WQEFCM16N_digital_email_20180110_SinemaRR_inact")
---

Peter,

Yesterday, Joe Arpaio -- the former Maricopa County sheriff notorious for his human rights abuses -- announced he's running for the U.S. Senate in Arizona.

Not only is he a birther, he was also found guilty of ignoring a court order directing him to stop racially profiling and detaining people of color. And sure enough, Donald Trump pardoned him.

Arpaio might fit in with the Republican Party of Trump and Roy Moore, but he has no business in the Senate.

If we're going to keep him out, we need to ramp up our support for Kyrsten Sinema, our candidate who's already hard at work running to turn this critical Senate seat blue.

**[Donate $3 right now to Kyrsten's campaign to make sure she wins the Senate seat in Arizona this November-- 100% of your donation will go directly to her.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6a22/24c69fd9/13a81db96/1892e811/4130463571/VEsE/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/kyrsten-sinema-18?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+right+now+to+Kyrstens+campaign+&utm_campaign=WQEFCM16N_digital_email_20180110_SinemaRR_inact&source=WQEFCM16N_digital_email_20180110_SinemaRR_inact")**

This is one of our best opportunities to turn a red Senate seat blue.

We endorsed Kyrsten in this race because we know that after a career dedicated to public service, she's the champion Arizona needs.

Helping Kyrsten now could make the difference between sending a proven fighter to the Senate or sending a bigot like Arpaio there.

We already saw how close that kind of fight can get from the recent Senate race in Alabama. So it's critical we send Kyrsten the resources to win.

**Donate $3 to Kyrsten and 100% of your donation goes directly to her campaign:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-Kyrsten-Sinema](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6a22/24c69fd9/13a81db96/1892e812/4130463571/VEsF/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/kyrsten-sinema-18?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportKyrstenSi&utm_campaign=WQEFCM16N_digital_email_20180110_SinemaRR_inact&source=WQEFCM16N_digital_email_20180110_SinemaRR_inact")**

Thanks for all you do,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Greitens?=
subject: Important Statements
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
Team,

I wanted to share with you some statements that are important in light of news you may have seen tonight.

Statement from James F. Bennett, our attorney

"The governor has now seen the TV report that ran tonight. The station declined to provide the tape or transcript in advance of running their story, which contained multiple false allegations. The claim that this nearly three-year old story has generated or should generate law enforcement interest is completely false. There was no “blackmail," and that claim is false. This personal matter has been addressed by the Governor and Mrs. Greitens privately years ago when it happened. The outrageous claims of improper conduct regarding these almost three-year-ago events are a lie.”

Joint Statement from Sheena and I

"A few years ago, before Eric was elected governor, there was a time when he was unfaithful in our marriage. This was a deeply personal mistake. Eric took responsibility, and we dealt with this together honestly and privately. While we never would have wished for this pain in our marriage, or the pain that this has caused others, with God’s mercy Sheena has forgiven and we have emerged stronger. We understand that there will be some people who cannot forgive – but for those who can find it in your heart, Eric asks for your forgiveness, and we are grateful for your love, your compassion, and your prayers."

Additional Statement from Sheena Greitens

"We have a loving marriage and an awesome family; anything beyond that is between us and God. I want the media and those who wish to peddle gossip to stay away from me and my children."

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on Facebook (https://facebook.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=2eabde3b08-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-2eabde3b08-99411629) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=2eabde3b08-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-2eabde3b08-99411629) . Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. If they become too much, you can unsubscribe (https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=2eabde3b08) .

name: The White House
subject: ‘You must be making good products’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdbf6a7f4a7dc780fa47a7833839c32b2113a01a4beda27ca945c1c4fe731afd97e29c39a4be2bfa436658d78173ccd00d1cfefdf040a055

‘You must be making good products’

President Donald J. Trump welcomed Norwegian Prime Minister Erna Solberg to the White House yesterday. The two leaders talked security, NATO, and trade and investment between the United States and Norway.

“You are our closest ally inside NATO,” Prime Minister Solberg told President Trump. Both she and the President also emphasized Norway’s importance as a major customer of American-made defense and technology products.

“In the last years, there has even been a surplus from the U.S. to Norway on the economic side,” the Prime Minister said. “So you must be making good products.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdbf6a7f4a7dc780c61104f267a30d4a0e2ecd92f183bfb6ab00deb53772f0a596007165729c775f14484f400c680793dd8cc875b0e959d3
White House video: Take a look inside the Prime Minister’s visit.

The first Cabinet meeting of 2018

“2017 was a year of tremendous achievement—monumental achievement, actually,” President Trump said as he kicked off the first official Cabinet meeting of the new year.

The President listed off numerous highlights from 2017 while charting a course for the coming weeks:

- “Since that tax cut was enacted, more than 1 million workers have already received a tax cut bonus.”
- “We’ve set a new record on reducing regulation and all forms of stopping growth . . . that were crippling America’s economy.”
- “Any [immigration] bill we pass must improve jobs, wages, and security for American citizens.”
- “I want to thank my Cabinet for working tirelessly on behalf of our country. Every single day, every hour.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdbf6a7f4a7dc7801056a7e6551f45900527c9a3fa8a998ad98d532ae07c329a6b13e81ecfa5aca6539d9fcfe6287856dad59f23e23cfc4d
Read what President Trump told his Cabinet to kick off 2018.

US Air Force: We have to be ‘faster and smarter’

“We’re changing the way we do business,” Air Force Secretary Heather Wilson said last summer as she unveiled plans to launch AFwerX—a new technology accelerator program that will help the Air Force find cutting-edge and affordable solutions to its tech challenges.

Today, Vice President Mike Pence travels to Las Vegas to celebrate the grand opening of that facility. Its launch is a testament to the strength of America’s small tech businesses and their entrepreneurial innovation—an economic advantage America enjoys that it can more aggressively translate into a national security asset, as well.

“We have to move faster and be smarter,” Secretary Wilson said. “We know there are people out there with answers to problems . . . We are going to look at what they have and help them help us.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdbf6a7f4a7dc780e179620a593eb687da63d2ee689c3fb5409faa2a4f2d8572e9d68f3e7dc305007ef41ab4fdb04e5a35cb92e49da42411
Listen to the Vice President’s remarks live at 5:45 p.m. ET today.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and Prime Minister Erna Solberg of Norway | January 10, 2018 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

This afternoon, President Trump will lead a prison reform roundtable. Later, The President will meet with his National Security team.

Following his remarks at the grand opening of AFwerX Vegas, the Vice President will deliver remarks to airmen and their families at Nellis Air Force Base.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdbf6a7f4a7dc7800ac17f93761c7fef83c37e6d6865e8c7cf0f72c006cd01d03f6101c83605f062ff49b899a5d58a20a7a576ce36b950bf
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=cdbf6a7f4a7dc780fc71e9b6b96cc21d2ffe150a87f4e5dd38b327cf10366266ec4412010a03ba8a8ef141aafdb2b6f55154d6e79f34b69b
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4270d14339b2206834558b50592be6509a76770cb1851fe0fd9b2b22d9d1823dcd465878e2cab22fe2fe0aa577bb5c769

subject: I need to ask you to do something important, Derek
body:
We need your help to invest in grassroots organizing in every single state.
---
Derek --

I'm about to ask you to make a monthly sustaining contribution to the Democratic Party. But first, I want to tell you why this request matters so much at this moment.

We just kicked off an election year. There are great Democratic candidates running in every part of this country -- and they need resources to help them get their message out and win.

Here's the truth, Derek. The investments we make during the next few months will determine wins and losses in November -- and we need your help to invest in grassroots organizing in every single state.

That's why I am asking:

**[Will you make a monthly sustaining donation today to help Democrats organize nationwide and win in November? Any amount you can contribute will make a difference.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522304/4067b0a/4a0a0cfc4/7e5ecb2d/1593705168/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dnc_monthly_sustainer?recurring=true&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+Will+you+make+a+monthly+sustaining+donat&utm_campaign=em_20180111_tp_nd&source=em_20180111_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180111_tp_nd")**

Democrats showed what we can accomplish together in 2017 -- but the truth is that our work is just getting started.

We can take back control of Congress and stop the Republican agenda in its tracks.

We can win back power at the local level -- including fighting for the 6,000 state legislature seats on the ballot this year.

We can continue building a party infrastructure that is capable of taking on the Republicans and beating them at the polls.

But the only way we will be able to do any of this if is we work together -- with lots of people chipping in what they can afford. So what do you say, Derek?

**[Help sustain this fight for the long haul with a monthly contribution today. Your contribution -- of any amount -- will be critical to helping Democrats win this year.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522304/4067b0a/4a0a0cfc4/7e5ecb2a/1593705168/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dnc_monthly_sustainer?recurring=true&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Help+sustain+this+fight+for+the+long+hau&utm_campaign=em_20180111_tp_nd&source=em_20180111_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180111_tp_nd")**

body:
My focus can be summed up in three words: people, people, people. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fee45df622)
---

|
**Governor Holcomb's 2018 State of the State:**
**_Big Picture, Long Term, Transforming Lives_**
---
| | Friends -

We've heard the story -- when he was traveling across the state in 2016, now-Governor Eric Holcomb made a basket in all of Indiana's 92 counties. And this week, he made a slam dunk when [he delivered his second State of the State address](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f6eaf31e23&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

For Governor Holcomb, priority one, two and three are the same -- _**_they're people, people, people_**. _

Governor Holcomb is committed to _**_transforming_**_ Hoosier lives -- whether it's by putting opioid treatment centers within an hour's drive of every Hoosier, helping underemployed Hoosiers gain the skills they need for in-demand, high-wage jobs, or protecting those most vulnerable -- our children -- by working to make Indiana the home of the lowest infant mortality rate in the Midwest!
**> >****[Donate NOW to help Governor Holcomb transform Hoosier lives](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=246de05d03&e=1acd9bb3d6)****< <**
Each Hoosier deserves the opportunity to __**live their life to the fullest, and Governor Holcomb has laid out ambitious, bold**_** _goals_**_ to help Hoosiers do just that!

Our governor said it best this week:
---
| | **"What a moment! It’s a moment we’ve built toward, a moment full of potential._It’s Indiana’s time._ We have an unprecedented opportunity to take Indiana to the Next Level and, along the way, help transform the lives of more Hoosier children, students and citizens than ever before."**
---
| Here at your Indiana Republican Party, in 2018 we're committed to __**sharing Governor Holcomb's message**__ and __**electing more Republicans**__ up and down the ballot to bring this bold vision to life for Hoosiers. Democrats are already trying to smear and disparage these important priorities, __**and we can't let that happen**__.

However, __**we can't do this without your help**__. That's why we're asking for you to [make a donation of $25, $50, $100, $250 or $500 now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3bc082c62c&e=1acd9bb3d6) to help us continue to build the Republican team that will deliver __**bold, transformative change**__ on the biggest issues facing Hoosiers in all corners of our state.

__**It's Indiana's time.**__ We've got the goals in place -- and it's going to take the full team, including Hoosiers like you -- to make it all possible.

Thanks,
Kyle Hupfer
Chairman, Indiana Republican Party

P.S. We're one year into Governor Holcomb's first term, and __**he needs to maintain and grow our strong Republican team**__ to help bring his five pillars to life for Hoosiers. Please consider [making a donation **NOW**](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1c69eccc28&e=1acd9bb3d6) to help us share his message and elect more Republicans _throughout_ Indiana.

subject: Florida's children are counting on us

body:
Friend,

Congressional Republicans are putting more than 374,000 Florida children at risk.

**Instead of doing the right thing at the end of last year and reauthorizing funding for the Children 's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), Republicans allowed it to expire.**

[**Demand Congress stop playing partisan games with the lives of nine million American children. Sign on here to demand immediate funding for CHIP >>>**](http://act.floridadems. "https://petitions.signforgood.com/fundchipnow/?code=FD&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-chip%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1%26amount%3D10.00%26recurring%3Dtrue&firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=1+-+Demand+Congress+stop+playing+partisan+ga&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1")

Millions of working families count on CHIP to provide health care for their children, whether it's for regular checkups or emergency services.

In Florida, CHIP funds the 'Florida KidCare' program, which provides high-quality, low-cost health insurance for children from birth to 18 across Florida.

But now, thanks to Republican inaction, funding is running out.

[**Demand Congress immediately pass funding for CHIP. Add your name here >>>**](http://act.floridadems. "https://petitions.signforgood.com/fundchipnow/?code=FD&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-chip%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1%26amount%3D10.00%26recurring%3Dtrue&firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+Demand+Congress+immediately+pass+funding&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1&source=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1&refcode=FDP_EM_PT_180DO_180111_1_1")

Health coverage for nearly nine million children across America is hanging in the balance, while Republicans continue to drag their feet.

Thanks,

Florida Democrats

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Something important you can do to advance Medicare for All
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
I believe, and I think you do too, that it is long past time for the United States to join the rest of the industrialized world in guaranteeing health care as a right to every man, woman, and child.
Rich or poor, we are all born, we all get sick, we all have accidents, we all need health care at the end of our lives, and we all die. And in my view, the function of a sane health care system in this country should be to keep all of our people well in a cost effective way, not to make health industry CEOs richer or to drive up the stock prices on Wall Street.
It wasn't long ago that the idea of Medicare for All was dismissed and ridiculed by the corporate media and political establishment in this country. Four years ago, I introduced the bill and there wasn't a single co-sponsor. We were actually relentlessly attacked for the idea before the Iowa Caucuses.
But then our political revolution really gained steam, and it became clear to candidates and elected officials alike that the American people were on board. They believe that it is the responsibility of the federal government to provide health care for all as a right, not a privilege.
And as a result of your passion, commitment and enthusiasm on this issue, when I introduced my Medicare for All legislation earlier this year, 16 of my colleagues in the Senate signed on as co-sponsors. I can tell you that no one would have ever thought that was possible just a short while ago.
**But if we want to add to that number, our job now is to support those who have come out in favor of our bill and defend them when they are under attack for doing-so. So I want to ask you to do something very important for our our cause today:**
**[Can you split a $3 donation between Elizabeth Warren's and Tammy Baldwin's campaigns for U.S. Senate? Elizabeth is running for re-election in Massachusetts and Tammy is running for re-election in Wisconsin. Both support our plan. Both have been attacked by Republicans for doing. Help them, and you help our effort to pass Medicare for All.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-mfa-candidates?refcode=bernie-em180111-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-mfa-candidates?refcode=bernie-em180111-full")**
My Republican colleagues in Congress tell us, over and over again, that we have the "greatest" health care system in the world.
Really?
Today, 28 million Americans have no health insurance and many more are underinsured with high deductibles and co-payments. We pay, by far, the highest prices in the world for prescription drugs. In fact, millions of Americans can't afford to purchase the medicine they need.
Too many Americans delay going to the doctor because of the cost, and too many others are forced into bankruptcy or financial ruin because of medical bills. It is not a radical idea to suggest that Americans should be able to get the health care they need, when they need it. Americans should not be dying because of their financial status.
There are millions of people who remain on their jobs today, not because they want to be there or enjoy their work, but because their present employer provides decent health care benefits - and they have no option.
Today the United States spends far more per capita on health care than any other nation on Earth. In fact, we are spending almost twice as much per capita as any other country, while our health care outcomes are often worse.
Instead of providing quality care to all in a cost-effective way, our current system is designed to provide hundreds of billions in profits to insurance companies, the pharmaceutical industry and medical equipment suppliers.
That system doesn't sound so "great" to me.
Medicare for All would mean comprehensive coverage for all Americans. It would cover the entire continuum of health care: inpatient to outpatient care; preventive to emergency care; primary to specialty care, including long-term and palliative care; vision, hearing and oral health care; mental health and substance abuse services; as well as prescription medications, medical equipment, supplies, diagnostics, and treatments. Patients will be able to choose a health care provider without worrying about whether that provider is in-network and will be able to get the care they need without having to read any fine print or trying to figure out how they can afford the out-of-pocket costs.
Of course, the insurance companies and drug companies in this country are extraordinarily powerful and they are not going down without a fight. They have obscene amounts of wealth and have used that money to protect a dysfunctional system that allows them to make billions in profits while leaving far too many Americans behind.
**They will spend obscene amounts of money to stop the movement for Medicare for All, and the candidates and elected officials who support it. But if we stand together, we will win. That's why it's so important for me to ask:**
**[Can you split a $3 donation between Elizabeth Warren's and Tammy Baldwin's campaigns for U.S. Senate?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-mfa-candidates?refcode=bernie-em180111-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-mfa-candidates?refcode=bernie-em180111-full")**
It is time to wage a moral and political war against a dysfunctional health care system in this country. And that means supporting those who stand with us when they are under attack for doing so.
Thank you for taking action in support of our campaign to guarantee health care as a right in this country for every man, woman and child.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

Too many Americans delay going to the doctor because of the cost, and too many others are forced into bankruptcy or financial ruin because of medical bills. It is not a radical idea to suggest that Americans should be able to get the health care they need, when they need it. Americans should not be dying because of their financial status. There are millions of people who remain on their jobs today, not because they want to be there or enjoy their work, but because their present employer provides decent health care benefits - and they have no option. Today the United States spends far more per capita on health care than any other nation on Earth. In fact, we are spending almost twice as much per capita as any other country, while our health care outcomes are often worse. Instead of providing quality care to all in a cost-effective way, our current system is designed to provide hundreds of billions in profits to insurance companies, the pharmaceutical industry and medical equipment suppliers. That system doesn't sound so "great" to me. Medicare for All would mean comprehensive coverage for all Americans. It would cover the entire continuum of health care: inpatient to outpatient care; preventive to emergency care; primary to specialty care, including long-term and palliative care; vision, hearing and oral health care; mental health and substance abuse services; as well as prescription medications, medical equipment, supplies, diagnostics, and treatments. Patients will be able to choose a health care provider without worrying about whether that provider is in-network and will be able to get the care they need without having to read any fine print or trying to figure out how they can afford the out-of-pocket costs. Of course, the insurance companies and drug companies in this country are extraordinarily powerful and they are not going down without a fight. They have obscene amounts of wealth and have used that money to protect a dysfunctional system that allows them to make billions in profits while leaving far too many Americans behind. They will spend obscene amounts of money to stop the movement for Medicare for All, and the candidates and elected officials who support it. But if we stand together, we will win. That's why it's so important for me to ask: "" It is time to wage a moral and political war against a dysfunctional health care system in this country. And that means supporting those who stand with us when they are under attack for doing so. Thank you for taking action in support of our campaign to guarantee health care as a right in this country for every man, woman and child. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders ** "" ** Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

subject: I want to know - what matters to you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |

| |
---
| These days, it's easy to get distracted by politics -- Trump's latest tweets, his spokesperson's most recent "alternative facts," or trying to figure out which Cabinet Secretary will get fired next. This is an Election Year, so the distractions will only get worse. **But we can't let ourselves be diverted from the issues that matter.** | [](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113faa/622b7b5b/18ae5f23/307bbb53/1707374365/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180107")
---
After all, a single mother working two jobs doesn't care about Twitter. She cares about paying the rent and putting food on the table for her children.
A senior living off Social Security doesn't care how many people really attended a Trump rally. They just need their checks to cover expenses.
Families in Florida don't care if Trump believes in climate change or not. They care that storms are getting more powerful and frequent, and they worry what will happen if they're in the path of the next one.
**Behind every policy issue, there are real people with real concerns.** Real families are affected by the decisions we make in Washington. We've just started a New Year, which means new priorities and new challenges to face.
So today, I want to hear from you. [_**Share what issue you're most passionate about, and what you'd like to see done about it in 2018.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113faa/622b7b5b/18ae5f23/307bbb53/1707374365/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180107")
I'm looking forward to your responses.
Debbie

| __
The clock is ticking on reaching a bipartisan budget agreement and avoiding a destructive government shutdown that would harm tens of millions of Americans.

Republicans remain determined to hold DREAMers hostage to their plans to spend an eye-popping $18 billion to build a wall that the overwhelming majority of Americans do not want. (In fact, Republicans are actually robbing the funding allocated for effective border security measures in order to waste taxpayer money on a long wall that won't work.)

And while big corporations and the wealthiest Americans have been struggling with what to do with their massive benefits from the GOP tax scam, 9 million American children and their families are still waiting for Republicans to agree to fully fund the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

[**This is outrageous, but not surprising. Congressional Republicans and President Trump have continued to show a massive lack of compassion for everyday people and the working men and women who will be directly affected by a government shutdown.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91867/70cdc2ba/46eaf7f4/3902123/3740293339/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/sign-demand-congress-protects-our-children?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+This+is+outrageous+but+not+surprising+Co&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180111&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180111")

Chris Van Hollen

subject: Re: class size
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Legislators are back in Raleigh for a special session -- I'm calling on them to fund smaller class sizes.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120362b/2fde7c66/477fb500/1569fec9/1709211353/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/republican-leaders-fix-class-size-funding-now?utm_campaign=em17_180111_class_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_180111_class_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")
---
Friend,

In 2016, Republican leaders in the General Assembly passed a law that required smaller class sizes for kindergarten-3rd grade classes.

This sounds good, but because they failed to provide the necessary additional funding, it's causing chaos.

I believe smaller class sizes can be a good thing, but you have to pay for it.

**[This week, legislators are back in Raleigh for a special session -- I'm calling on them to fix this problem and fund smaller class sizes. Add your name to join me.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120362b/2fde7c66/477fb500/1569fec9/1709211353/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/republican-leaders-fix-class-size-funding-now?utm_campaign=em17_180111_class_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_180111_class_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")**

During the last two weeks, I've heard from school administrators, teachers, and concerned parents about this change.

We should be helping schools hire highly qualified teachers, not forcing them to resort to measures that will set our students back.

Republican legislative leaders created this problem; now I'm calling on them to fix it during this special session.

**[Add your name if you agree they should provide funding for smaller class sizes.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120362b/2fde7c66/477fb500/1569fec9/1709211353/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/republican-leaders-fix-class-size-funding-now?utm_campaign=em17_180111_class_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_180111_class_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")**

Thanks for all you continue to do,

Roy

| Dear Peter, Roughly 25 million Christians do not vote in Presidential Elections, and an astounding 51 million Christians will not vote in the 2018 midterm elections. You can change these staggering statistics. [I’m asking you to prayerfully consider making a donation to My Faith Votes today.](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/180111apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180111_apl_s1&n=2) Look at what April recently wrote on the My Faith Votes Facebook page – she perfectly sums up the problem. _“This past voting session in Texas I worked at ‘early voting.’ The turnout was disturbingly low. Even those who came in to vote did not know what the voting was about and had to return at a later day so they could study the issues first._ ___I realize now how unhelpful, even hurtful laws can pass that most people don't want .... simply not enough people show up to vote.”_
-April H. Unfortunately, Christians in America have been silent at the voting booth. **We must reverse this disturbing trend this year in the primary and midterm elections.** The only way this **CAN** be accomplished is by engaging more Christians in the voting process and equipping them to vote for Christian values. Together, we can do this. We have the plan and the tools. You and I must blaze a path with our resources and our influence – to reach more Christians and to preserve an America that secures peace and prosperity for our children and our grandchildren. [So please, make your best gift to My Faith Votes today to reach more Christians before the primary midterm elections!](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/180111apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180111_apl_s1&n=3) God Bless you. **United. We Stand.**

Mike Huckabee

subject: Update re: Important Statements
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
All,

We wanted to send you this new statement regarding a new, ridiculous false story that actually reveals the motivation behind these lies.

Statement regarding most recent allegations - James F. Bennett

"We have been asked repeatedly by reputable news outlets why we believe this nearly three-year-old news story is coming out now. The latest reporting has finally disclosed that the reporting was being driven by a "source" who is the former Democrat state party chairman and who apparently has not spoken to the person in question. This goes a long way to explaining what is going on - this is a political hit piece.

This is and remains an almost three-year-old private matter with no matter of public interest at stake. Eric and Sheena have worked through those issues long ago and I think that Sheena put it best: 'We have a loving marriage and an awesome family; anything beyond that is between us and God. I want the media and those who wish to peddle gossip to stay away from me and my children.' Now we know who has been peddling that gossip."

###

- Team Greitens

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Eric Greitens
Date: Wed, Jan 10, 2018 at 11:58 PM
Subject: Important Statements

Team,

I wanted to share with you some statements that are important in light of news you may have seen.

Statement from James F. Bennett, our attorney

"The governor has now seen the TV report that ran tonight. The station declined to provide the tape or transcript in advance of running their story, which contained multiple false allegations. The claim that this nearly three-year old story has generated or should generate law enforcement interest is completely false. There was no “blackmail," and that claim is false. This personal matter has been addressed by the Governor and Mrs. Greitens privately years ago when it happened. The outrageous claims of improper conduct regarding these almost three-year-ago events are a lie.”

Joint Statement from Sheena and I

"A few years ago, before Eric was elected governor, there was a time when he was unfaithful in our marriage. This was a deeply personal mistake. Eric took responsibility, and we dealt with this together honestly and privately. While we never would have wished for this pain in our marriage, or the pain that this has caused others, with God’s mercy Sheena has forgiven and we have emerged stronger. We understand that there will be some people who cannot forgive – but for those who can find it in your heart, Eric asks for your forgiveness, and we are grateful for your love, your compassion, and your prayers."

Additional Statement from Sheena Greitens

"We have a loving marriage and an awesome family; anything beyond that is between us and God. I want the media and those who wish to peddle gossip to stay away from me and my children."

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. name: Pence Policy Team
subject: Official 2018 Team Pence Priorities Survey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg Pence for Congress |

body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfe5/2a380d31/6b8c6c8/f1c08d/2451797349/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfe5/2a380d31/6b8c6c8/f1c08d/2451797349/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hi there --
Republicans recently passed a scam tax bill that adds $1.5 trillion to the national debt, all while giving corporate billionaires a tax break.
The worst part? While Mitch McConnell's wealthy campaign backers get a tax break, 9 million American children and their families are still waiting for Republicans to fully fund the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).
Senate Republicans' priorities are out of whack, and we need to hold them accountable. [**Please sign our petition and demand that Congress act now to restore CHIP and ensure that our children have the resources they need to get and stay healthy.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfe5/2a380d31/6b8c6c8/f1c08e/2451797349/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/fundchipnow/?code=hassan&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2018_fundchip_up&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Funding CHIP for five years would cost around 0.1% of the total cost of the Republican tax scam, yet the GOP is holding health care for millions of children hostage. It's been months since Republicans in Congress let CHIP expire, longer than it has ever gone without being reauthorized.
CHIP has historically had widespread bipartisan support but now, Republican leaders in both the House and Senate are refusing to extend the program. **This is urgent, everyone -- children in some states could lose coverage as soon as next Friday.**
[**If you believe that Congress should care as much about health care for children as they do about tax breaks for Wall Street millionaires, please sign our grassroots petition and demand that CHIP be fully funded.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfe5/2a380d31/6b8c6c8/f1c08e/2451797349/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/fundchipnow/?code=hassan&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2018_fundchip_up&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you for standing up for children,
Team Maggie

| **Gov. Holcomb Delivers 2018 State of the State Address** In case you missed it, this week Governor Eric Holcomb delivered his second State of the State address. It focused on **people, people, people** \-- and set **bold, transformative goals** to make life better for __all_ _Hoosiers.

| | **"2018 is all about taking the tools that we’ve been provided and putting them to work with a single purpose in mind—to make life better for all Hoosiers. That’s why this year, my focus can be summed up in three words:_people, people, people_."
-Governor Eric Holcomb**
---
| | |
---
| _**What They're Saying:**_
_"Governor Holcomb delivered a bold, people-focused message to Hoosiers as he laid out a blueprint to build on the significant progress we have made over the past year. As we look toward the future, we will provide quality training to ensure our workforce matches Indiana’s ever-growing and evolving economy, attack the drug epidemic and provide help to those suffering from addiction."_
**-Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**

_"Eric Holcomb is one of the most genuine public elected officials that I've had the opportunity to work with over the last thirty years, and I think that really came across this evening... Some of the goals that the Governor set for Hoosier workforce and Hoosier health are long-term goals, and I think that that is the mark of an outstanding public servant as well...But to be a true visionary leader, you have to look long-term, you have to plan long-term."_
**-House Speaker Brian Bosma**

_“Some of the problems that have plagued us in the past as a state -- we are getting those fixed. Governor Holcomb is leading the way on that.”_
**-Senate Presisdent Pro Tem David Long**

_"Governor Eric Holcomb articulated a bold vision for Indiana in his second State of the State, a vision that focuses first, second and third on people. His first year in office was marked by incredible and transformational successes that will benefit our state for a generation to come. And now, he aims to build upon that momentum by focusing his efforts squarely on Indiana’s workforce, tackling the opioid epidemic and continued economic growth and infrastructure development."_
**-Kyle Hupfer, Chairman of the Indiana Republican Party**
_"We applaud Governor Holcomb for this evening’s remarks. Not only is he committed to empowering the small business community by ensuring that the economy is sound and prosperous, but he is clearly determined to develop the Hoosier workforce...Tonight, the Governor demonstrated his long-term commitment to ensuring that our job creators will have a cultivated and dedicated workforce."_
**-Barbara Quandt Underwood, State Director, National Federation of Independent Business **
